---
title: "Ten Simple Rules for Turning Bioinformatics Analyses into Interactive Web Applications"
page-layout: article
toc: false
---

## Companion website

This website accompanies the manuscript:

> **Ten Simple Rules for Turning Bioinformatics Analyses into Interactive Web Applications**  
> Agudelo-Romero P, Caparros-Martin J, Sikazwe C, Blyth C.  
> [Journal and DOI].

The manuscript presents ten general rules for converting a bioinformatics analysis into a shareable interactive web application. This site provides the worked example, expanded implementation notes, supplementary code examples, and reproducibility material that were moved out of the main paper for brevity.

## Worked example

The worked example used throughout the manuscript is an interactive application for differential gene expression exploration and Gene Ontology enrichment. Two implementations are provided:

- an **R Shiny** implementation, used as the reference implementation for the genomics workflow
- a **Python Shiny** implementation, included as a parallel worked example illustrating the same design principles in a different ecosystem

Both applications accept a CSV derived from differential expression analysis and support interactive thresholding, visualisation, enrichment analysis, and downloadable reporting.

The validation dataset used in the worked example is **GSE60450**.

## Launch the apps

### R Shiny

Reference implementation for the worked genomics example, using R Shiny and Bioconductor-based tooling.

[Open R app →](https://YOUR_USERNAME.shinyapps.io/YOUR_APP)

### Python Shiny

Parallel implementation illustrating the same design principles in Python Shiny.

[Open Python app →](https://huggingface.co/spaces/YOUR_USERNAME/YOUR_APP)

## What this site provides

- **Methods**: technical context for the worked example, including dataset choice and implementation notes
- **Supplementary Code Examples**: expanded paired snippets corresponding to Figures 2 and 3 in the manuscript
- **Reproducibility**: environment restoration, setup, local execution, and deployment notes
- **Availability**: data, code, repository, and citation information

## Source code

All source code for the manuscript companion materials and both application implementations is available in the public GitHub repository:

[GitHub repository](https://github.com/agudeloromero/limma-deg-explorer)

## Citation

If you use the code or applications, please cite the manuscript when bibliographic details become available.

> Agudelo-Romero P, Caparros-Martin J, Sikazwe C, Blyth C.  
> **Ten Simple Rules for Turning Bioinformatics Analyses into Interactive Web Applications.**  
> [Journal and DOI].

## Acknowledgements

Patricia Agudelo-Romero
The Kids Research Institute Australia

Validation dataset: **GSE60450**.
